# OHIP Province and Territory Codes

> **Source**: [https://www.health.gov.on.ca/en/pro/programs/ohip/claims_submission/province_and_territory_codes.pdf]
> **Fetched**: 2026-04-29
> **Pages**: 3
> **Format**: extracted text from PDF via `pypdfium2`; rendered as a verbatim text block. Multi-column tables in the PDF do not extract cleanly with pypdfium2 - refer to the PDF for the canonical table layout.
> **Authoritative source**: the PDF at the URL above. If this MD and the PDF disagree, the PDF wins.

---

```
A province or territory code is required for reciprocal claims to identify the province or
territory of the patient’s registration/address.
Province or Territory Province
Code Format Prior format
Alberta AB 9 numerics-individual
registration (effective June 1,
1994)
Prior to June 1,
1994, 11
numerics
British Columbia BC 10 numerics-individual
registration
Manitoba MB 9 numerics – individual
registration (effective April 1,
2005)
Prior to April 1,
2005, 6
numerics
Newfoundland and
Labrador
NL 12 numerics-individual
registration
New Brunswick NB 9 numerics-individual
registration
Northwest Territories NT 8 characters-individual
registration. One alpha (D, N,
H, M or T and 7 numerics)
Nova Scotia NS 10 numerics-individual
registration (effective January
1, 1994)
Prior to January
1, 1994, 11
numerics
(Family Based)
Prince Edward Island PE 8 numerics (SIN)-individual
registration
Quebec* PQ 12 characters-individual. 4
alpha and 8 numerics
Saskatchewan SK 9 numerics-individual
registration
Territory of Nunavut NU 9 numerics-individual
registration
Yukon YT 9 numerics-individual
registration

*Note: The Province of Quebec does not participate fully in the Reciprocal Medical
Billing System. Claims for Quebec residents cannot be submitted on Electronic Input,
with the exception of claims eligible for submission through the Outaouais program.
Exemption
This technical publication has been exempted from translation under the French
Language Services Act as per Ontario Regulation 671/92.
```
